Create test/test_helper/common.bash with shared setup (PATH stubs for gh, fixture paths). Create test/fixtures/workflows/ and test/fixtures/api_responses/ directories. Add .shellcheckrc. {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-5vc","title":"Fix fork ref detection — add github.head_ref and head.repo.full_name","description":"Line 285: Pattern only matches pull_request.head.(sha|ref). Misses github.head_ref (equivalent in PRT context) and pull_request.head.repo.full_name (used with actions/checkout repository: param). These are common dangerous checkout patterns classified as low-risk. Add to the regex.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:14Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:14Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-vek","title":"Fix base64 -d portability — fails silently on stock macOS","description":"Line 217: base64 -d is GNU coreutils flag. Stock macOS uses -D. The 2\u003e/dev/null || true masks the failure, so all workflow files end up empty on macOS. Detect the correct flag at script startup. This is the highest priority fix.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:20Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:20Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-di0","title":"Add trap EXIT for temp file cleanup","description":"Lines 237, 385, 369: Script creates temp files via mktemp but has no trap handler. If killed (Ctrl-C, SIGTERM, set -e failure), files are orphaned in /tmp. Add: trap cleanup EXIT with a function that removes TABLE_ROWS, TABLE_ROWS.csv, ORG_SECRETS_FILE, and optionally AUDIT_DIR.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:20Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:20Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-das","title":"Add python3 preflight check","description":"Lines 160-168: Preflight checks verify gh CLI but not python3, which is a listed dependency. If python3 is missing, Phase 4 silently produces 'unknown' for all org settings. Add command -v python3 check alongside the gh check. (Or better: eliminate python3 dependency by using gh api --jq — see related DRY issue.)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z"}

new_value: Added python3 preflight check with command -v, test passes

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-di9","title":"Add ORG name validation","description":"Line 104-111: ORG is used unsanitized in file paths, API URLs, sed patterns, and markdown output. GitHub org names are restricted to [a-zA-Z0-9_-]. Validate with: [[ $ORG =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]] || exit 1. Prevents path traversal, sed delimiter injection, and malformed API calls.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:20Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:20Z"}

new_value: Added regex validation for ORG name [a-zA-Z0-9_-], prevents path traversal and injection

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-d2x","title":"Warn on API failures instead of silently swallowing","description":"Lines 193, 204, 215, 360, 387, 435, 439: All gh api calls use 2\u003e/dev/null || true, making HTTP 403, 404, network errors, and rate limiting indistinguishable from 'no data'. At minimum, check exit codes and emit warn() messages so users know data is missing from the report.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z"}

new_value: Added warn() calls to all gh api sites: user identity, repo secrets, org secrets, org permissions (2 calls). Silent fallbacks replaced with explicit warnings.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-kpd","title":"Fix secret name partial matching — FOO matches FOOBAR","description":"Line 415: grep -rl 'secrets.$secret_name' matches secrets.FOO inside secrets.FOOBAR. This inflates the 'Referenced In Workflows' column and generates incorrect gh secret set --repos remediation commands. Fix: use word-boundary pattern like secrets\\.$secret_name([^A-Za-z0-9_]|$) with grep -E.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z"}

new_value: Fixed grep to use word boundary: secrets.$name([^a-zA-Z0-9_]|$) prevents FOO matching FOOBAR

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-g3n","title":"Fix author_association false positives — require if: context","description":"Line 288: grep -q 'author_association' matches the string anywhere in the file — comments, echo statements, logging, non-conditional references. A workflow that logs author_association but doesn't gate on it gets classified as 'has guard'. Mitigation: strip comment lines before matching, or require 'if:' on the same/preceding line.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z"}

new_value: Fixed author_association and permissions: grep to strip comment lines before matching. Also fixed permissions-in-comment false positive (same class of bug).

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-ew1","title":"Warn when gh repo list hits 1000 limit","description":"Line 193: gh repo list --limit 1000 silently truncates. If exactly 1000 results are returned, warn the user that repos may be missing. Consider using --paginate or multiple calls for large orgs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-zbj","title":"Fix unquoted word-splitting loops and printf format strings","description":"Line 201: for repo in $all_repos — use while read loop. Line 214: for wf in $wf_list — same. Lines 149-153: Variables in printf format strings (SC2059). Line 672: rm -rf $AUDIT_DIR should use ${AUDIT_DIR:?}. These prevent breakage on repos/files with spaces or glob chars.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:55:21Z"}

new_value: Added warning when gh repo list returns 1000 results (limit hit)
Replaced for-in word splitting loops with while-read for repos and workflow files
All 8 children closed: mktemp, trap EXIT, base64 portability, python3 preflight, ORG validation, API warnings, 1000 limit warning, while-read loops

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-0dy","title":"Replace python3 JSON parsing with gh api --jq","description":"Lines 435-440: Three python3 -c invocations for JSON field extraction. gh api already supports --jq which is used elsewhere in the script. Refactoring to use --jq eliminates the python3 runtime dependency entirely. Related: python3 preflight check becomes unnecessary.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z"}

new_value: Replaced all 3 python3 JSON parsing calls with gh api --jq. Removed python3 as runtime dependency. Updated docs, tests, and preflight checks.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-dj6","title":"Read workflow files once into variable instead of 10 separate greps","description":"Lines 254, 274, 284-288, 331-336: Each workflow file is grepped up to 10 times (permissions, PRT, checkout, fork ref, dependabot, author guard x2, issue_comment, author_association, actor). Read file content once into a variable, then use [[ $content == *pattern* ]] bash matching. Eliminates ~10 forks per file.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z"}

new_value: Refactored Phase 2 to single-pass per-file: read wf_content once, strip comments to wf_uncommented once, then use bash matching and grep on variable. Reduced from ~10 grep-on-file calls to 1 cat + grep pipeline per file.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-x0t","title":"Extract analyze_repo() function from Phase 2 loop body","description":"Lines 239-371: The for-repo loop body is ~130 lines with ~20 variables leaking into global scope. Extract into analyze_repo() to enable local scoping and testability. This is the foundational refactor — classify_prt and classify_ic extraction depend on this.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z"}

new_value: Extracted analyze_repo() function with all local variables. Loop body reduced from ~130 lines to 5. Function outputs pipe-delimited rows to stdout.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-686","title":"Single-pass secret-to-repo mapping with associative array","description":"Line 415: grep -rl runs across all workflow files for EACH org secret. For 20 secrets and 500 files = 10,000 file reads. Instead: read all files once, extract secrets.XXX references, build declare -A associative array mapping secret names to repos. Reduces to O(files) from O(secrets*files).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z"}

new_value: Single-pass file-based secret mapping replaces O(N×M) grep loop. Uses temp file instead of associative array for bash 3.2 compatibility. Also lowered bash requirement from 4.0 to 3.2 (stock macOS).

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-4am","title":"Remove or justify sleep 0.1 in download loop","description":"Line 223: sleep 0.1 per repo adds ~100ms * repo_count of dead time (20 seconds for 200 repos). gh CLI handles rate limiting via retry headers. Either remove the sleep or replace with rate-limit header inspection. Add a comment if intentionally kept.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z"}

new_value: Removed sleep 0.1 — gh api handles its own rate limiting and retry. The sleep just slowed scans unnecessarily.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-5p7","title":"Replace echo-pipe with here-strings and eliminate unnecessary forks","description":"Lines 194, 364, 436-440: echo $var | cmd forks unnecessary subshells. Use here-strings: cmd \u003c\u003c\u003c $var. Lines 185, 275: basename in loops — use ${var##*/} parameter expansion. Lines 319-324: $(printf ...) subshells for string concat — use direct assignment. Lines 244-246: while-read-append — use mapfile.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z"}

new_value: Replaced 10 echo-pipe patterns with here-strings (<<<) in analyze_repo and download loop. Eliminates unnecessary subshell forks.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-hq7","title":"Extract classify_prt() and classify_ic() functions","description":"Lines 270-313 (PRT classifier) and 327-356 (IC classifier) are cohesive blocks that take a workflow file and return a classification string. Extract as separate functions for independent testability. Depends on analyze_repo() extraction.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z"}

new_value: Extracted classify_prt() and classify_ic() with 6 unit tests. analyze_repo() now calls them, reducing its body further.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-19m","title":"Reduce comment/string false positives for trigger detection","description":"Lines 254, 274, 331: Bare substring grep matches pull_request_target, issue_comment, and permissions: in YAML comments, run: block strings, and name: fields. Mitigation: strip lines starting with # before pattern matching (grep -v '^\\s*#'). Full fix requires YAML parsing but comment stripping catches the most common false positives.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z"}

new_value: Use wf_uncommented for pull_request_target and issue_comment trigger detection in analyze_repo(). Added fixtures prt-in-comment.yml and ic-in-comment.yml with 4 new tests.

Extract join_cells() function taking array, md separator, csv separator. Eliminates ~20 duplicated lines.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z"}

Lines 686-691 vs 697-702: Reuse command echoed in both interactive/non-interactive branches. Extract handle_cleanup() function to clarify main script flow.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:39Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:39Z"}

new_value: Extracted handle_cache_cleanup() function. Reduces main script flow from ~45 lines to a single function call.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-cfw","title":"Extract argument validation helper function","description":"Lines 74-94: Three identical 6-line blocks validating --local, --out, --csv arguments. Extract require_arg() function that validates next arg exists and does not start with -. Reduces to three 1-line calls.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:38Z"}

new_value: Extracted require_arg() function. Replaces 3 identical 5-line validation blocks with 1-line calls. 3 unit tests added.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-6vr","title":"Use process substitution for sort-pipe-while loops","description":"Lines 514, 546, 623, 636: sort FILE | while read runs the while body in a subshell. Not a bug today (loops only append to files) but fragile. Use: while read ... done \u003c \u003c(sort FILE) for consistency with the rest of the script and to avoid future variable-scoping surprises.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:48Z"}

new_value: Replaced 4 sort-pipe-while loops with process substitution (< <(sort FILE)) to avoid subshell variable scoping.

Clarify prt/ic abbreviations (prt_workflows_md, ic_workflows_md or similar). Standardize SCREAMING_CASE for config/paths, lowercase for runtime data. Initialize detail and detail_csv before if/elif chains.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:39Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:56:39Z"}

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-8p2","title":"Detect commented-out actions/checkout as false positive","description":"Line 284: grep -q 'actions/checkout' matches inside comments like '# - uses: actions/checkout@v4 # disabled'. The workflow gets classified as having a checkout when it doesn't. Same mitigation as comment stripping for trigger detection.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z","created_by":"alippold","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:57:15Z"}

All 5 children closed: sleep removal, here-strings, single-pass secrets, process substitution, single-file-read.
All 4 sub-epics closed: ki2 (security), 8lf (DRY), 5ad (heuristics), cot (performance). 80 tests, all passing.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-exh","title":"Detect expression injection in run: blocks","description":"Scan for ${{ github.event.* }} and ${{ github.head_ref }} interpolated directly into run: blocks. This is the #1 exploited GHA vulnerability class (Ultralytics, Nx, hackerbot-claw). README lists it as attack vector #3 but the tool doesn't detect it. Dangerous contexts: issue.title, issue.body, pull_request.title, pull_request.body, pull_request.head.ref, comment.body, review.body, commits[*].message, head_ref. Add classify_expr_injection() function following classify_prt/classify_ic pattern. Add new column to per-repo report table.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:03Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:03Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-e2w","title":"Detect unpinned action references (tag vs SHA)","description":"Detect actions referenced by mutable tag (uses: foo/bar@v4) vs immutable SHA (uses: foo/bar@692973e3d...). This was the exact vector in the tj-actions/changed-files compromise (23K+ repos). Implementation: grep for uses: lines without @[0-9a-f]{40}. Report as ratio per repo (e.g., '2/15 SHA-pinned'). Flag third-party unpinned as higher risk than actions/* unpinned. GitHub now supports SHA pinning enforcement via org policy (Aug 2025).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:06Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:06Z"}

new_value: Added classify_unpinned() function, 4 fixtures, 14 tests (grep, unit, integration). New Unpinned Actions column in md+csv reports.

Added classify_expr_injection() with awk-based run: block extraction, 7 fixtures, 15 tests (grep, unit, integration). New Expression Injection column in md+csv reports. Detects dangerous github.event.* and head_ref in run: blocks, ignores env: block usage and comments.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-6c6","title":"Detect self-hosted runner usage","description":"Detect runs-on: self-hosted or custom labels (not ubuntu-*/macos-*/windows-*). Self-hosted runners are persistent machines -- compromised runners give attackers access to host network and cached credentials. Critical when combined with pull_request_target, issue_comment, or workflow_run triggers. Flag in report with severity note.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:15Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:15Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-zys","title":"Detect dangerous permissions values (write-all, contents:write)","description":"Currently the tool checks whether a permissions: block EXISTS but not what it GRANTS. A workflow with permissions: write-all is just as dangerous as no permissions block. Detect: write-all, contents:write, actions:write, packages:write, id-token:write. Change permissions cell from 'All (3/3)' to include dangerous grants like 'All (3/3) -- 1 write-all'. Also detect id-token:write combined with pull_request_target (OIDC token theft risk).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:19Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:19Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-8yr","title":"Detect workflow_run trigger and artifact poisoning","description":"Add detection for workflow_run trigger, which bypasses fork PR restrictions. Sub-classify: workflow_run + download-artifact = HIGH risk (artifact poisoning, used in Ultralytics YOLO attack), workflow_run + checkout = MEDIUM risk, workflow_run alone = LOW risk. Add classify_wfr() function following classify_prt pattern. Add new column or merge into trigger analysis.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:12Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:12Z"}

new_value: Added classify functions, fixtures, and tests for all three detection types. Integrated into analyze_repo with new report columns.
Added classify functions, fixtures, and tests for all three detection types. Integrated into analyze_repo with new report columns.
Added classify functions, fixtures, and tests for all three detection types. Integrated into analyze_repo with new report columns.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-ct5","title":"Add explicit error handling for gh repo list API failure","description":"Line 282: gh repo list failure is silently swallowed by 2\u003e/dev/null. If the API fails (network drop, auth expiry, rate limit), the script proceeds with 0 repos and produces a silently empty report. This is a security tool -- missing data is worse than a crash. Fix: exit with crit() if gh repo list returns non-zero. Also track download failures in Phase 1 loop and warn if repos failed to download.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:27Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:27Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-i45","title":"Deduplicate Phase 4 API call (permissions/workflow endpoint)","description":"Lines 597-604: The same endpoint orgs/ORG/actions/permissions/workflow is called twice with different --jq filters. Wastes an API call, doubles rate-limit cost, introduces inconsistency if one call fails. Fix: fetch once, extract both default_workflow_permissions and can_approve_pull_request_reviews from the same response. Also remove dead code: the 'True' (capital T) check on line 645 is unreachable since jq always returns lowercase 'true'.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:32Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:32Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-1rx","title":"Refactor analyze_repo output to eliminate fragile head/tail split","description":"Lines 501-505: analyze_repo outputs two lines (md + csv) split by head -1 / tail -1. This breaks if any cell contains a newline. Options: (1) write directly to temp files from within analyze_repo (pass file paths as args), (2) use NUL delimiter, (3) validate line count before splitting. Option 1 is cleanest -- eliminates the fragile two-line protocol entirely.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:35Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:35Z"}

new_value: Fixed: repo list error handling exits with crit, Phase 4 single API call, analyze_repo writes directly to files.
Fixed: repo list error handling exits with crit, Phase 4 single API call, analyze_repo writes directly to files.
Fixed: repo list error handling exits with crit, Phase 4 single API call, analyze_repo writes directly to files.

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-hxf","title":"Use grep -F for secret name lookup in SECRET_MAP_FILE","description":"Line 570: grep '^secret_name|' uses regex matching. GitHub secret names are [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* so no metacharacters in practice, but defense in depth says use grep -F (fixed string) or awk with exact field match. Alternative: validate secret names from API response before use.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:03Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:03Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-3yn","title":"Extract find_workflow_files() helper","description":"The same find expression appears 3 times (lines 267, 415, 543): find DIR -type f \\( -name '*.yml' -o -name '*.yaml' \\). Extract to a helper function for DRY and easier future changes (e.g., supporting .yml.j2).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:11Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:11Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-9j6","title":"Raise repo list limit and add --cleanup + --local warning","description":"Three minor fixes: (1) Raise gh repo list --limit from 1000 to 99999 for large orgs (google, microsoft, aws have 1000+ repos). (2) Warn when --cleanup is combined with --local (cleanup is silently ignored). (3) Remove dead code: 'True' (capital T) check in can_approve_prs comparison -- jq always returns lowercase 'true'.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:26Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:26Z"}

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-zbm","title":"Add workflow file URLs to report","description":"Include clickable GitHub URLs to each flagged workflow file: https://github.com/ORG/REPO/blob/HEAD/.github/workflows/FILENAME. URL is deterministic from org/repo/filename. Saves reviewers significant time navigating to flagged files in large orgs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:47Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:47Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-lky","title":"Add summary statistics section to report","description":"Add a summary at the top of the report with headline numbers: repos scanned, repos with no permissions blocks (count + %), repos with pull_request_target (by sub-classification), repos with issue_comment (no gate), org secrets with All visibility. Executives and security leads need the headline numbers without reading the full table.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:44Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:44Z"}

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-8aa","title":"Detect workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch input injection","description":"Detect workflow_dispatch inputs and repository_dispatch client_payload used in run: blocks via ${{ inputs.* }} or ${{ github.event.client_payload.* }}. These are injection vectors when repos have broad contributor access. Lower risk than expression injection (requires repo write access) but still a privilege escalation path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:44Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:44Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-c92","title":"Fix progress line clearing and reuse command quoting","description":"Three issues: (1) printf '%-80s' ' ' for clearing progress is a magic number that doesn't work on wider terminals -- use printf '\\r\\033[K' or tput. (2) Reuse command in handle_cache_cleanup doesn't quote paths with spaces. (3) paste delimiter inconsistency: line 485 uses ', ' (comma-space) while lines 561/571 use ',' (comma only). Standardize.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:19Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:19Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-8et","title":"Return single value from classify functions, strip markdown at call site","description":"classify_prt and classify_ic both produce detail|detail_csv where values differ only in markdown bold (**...**). Several branches have identical values. Return a single canonical value and use a strip_md() helper (sed 's/\\*\\*//g') to produce the CSV form. Reduces maintenance burden when adding new classifications.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:14Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:14Z"}

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-ub8","title":"Detect hardcoded secrets and harden-runner adoption","description":"Two low-effort checks: (1) Detect hardcoded tokens in workflow files (ghp_*, gho_*, AKIA* patterns) -- the SpotBugs attack started with a PAT in a workflow file. (2) Check whether repos use step-security/harden-runner -- it successfully detected the tj-actions compromise in real-time. Report as summary statistic: 'X/Y repos use harden-runner'.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:23Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:23Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-3zo","title":"Fix false positive: trigger keywords in run: string values","description":"A workflow with 'echo This uses pull_request_target' in a run: block is flagged as having a pull_request_target trigger. The current comment stripping (grep -v '^\\s*#') only handles full-line comments, not string values in run: blocks. Create fixture prt-in-string-value.yml and ic-in-string-value.yml. Consider checking if the keyword appears in the on: section specifically rather than anywhere in the file.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:51Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:51Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-5mv","title":"Fix mock gh CLI hash mismatch for Phase 4 tests","description":"The mock gh script hashes all args to find canned responses. But Phase 4 calls include --jq flags that are not included in mock_gh_response registrations. The hash mismatch means mock falls through to default exit 0 with empty output, and the || { warn; var=unknown } fallback catches it. Phase 4 report values are effectively untested. Fix: register mock responses with full arg list including --jq. Add assertions for org settings values in report.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:00Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:46:00Z"}

{"id":"gh-action-security-audit-aov","title":"Add CSV output tests and csv_field() unit tests","description":"Zero tests for CSV output generation. The csv_field() function (lines 772-781) handles quoting for commas, double quotes, and newlines -- none tested. Also csv_field is defined inside an if block so cannot be extracted via _script_preamble. Tests needed: (1) --csv flag produces valid CSV, (2) CSV has header row, (3) csv_field quotes commas, (4) csv_field escapes double quotes, (5) CSV has both per-repo and org-secrets sections separated by blank row.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:55Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T02:45:55Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Audit and fix CLI UX against GNU Coding Standards, POSIX conventions, and 12 Factor CLI (https://medium.com/@jdxcode/12-factor-cli-apps-dd3c227a0e46): (1) Support short + long options for common flags: -o/--out, -l/--local, -q/--quiet, -v/--verbose, -V/--version. (2) Support -- to end option processing. (3) --version prints version and exits. (4) Errors go to stderr, output to stdout. (5) Exit codes: 0=success, 1=findings detected (with --exit-code), 2=usage error. (6) Respect NO_COLOR env var (https://no-color.org). (7) Consider accepting ORG via -O/--org as well as positional. (8) 12-factor: prefer flags over args, show help when run with no args, use tables for dense info, disposition chars for success/failure. Reference: GNU argp conventions, POSIX utility conventions, 12 Factor CLI Apps."}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-x6s","title":"Align CLI with GNU/POSIX conventions","description":"Audit and fix CLI UX against GNU Coding Standards and POSIX conventions: (1) Support short + long options for common flags: -o/--out, -l/--local, -q/--quiet, -v/--verbose, -V/--version. (2) Support -- to end option processing. (3) --version prints version and exits. (4) Errors go to stderr, output to stdout. (5) Exit codes: 0=success, 1=findings detected (with --exit-code), 2=usage error. (6) Respect NO_COLOR env var (https://no-color.org). (7) Consider accepting ORG via -O/--org as well as positional. Reference: GNU argp conventions, POSIX utility conventions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:22:31Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:22:31Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Since this is a GitHub Actions tool in the gh ecosystem, align with gh CLI conventions rather than generic GNU/POSIX. Reference: gh CLI source, gh extension docs, 12 Factor CLI. Key conventions: (1) --json flag with structured output (matches gh pr list --json). (2) --jq flag for filtering JSON output (matches gh api --jq). (3) --format for choosing output (table, json, csv). (4) Default to human-readable table output on TTY, JSON on pipe. (5) Respect GH_FORCE_TTY and NO_COLOR env vars. (6) Short flags for common options (-o/--out, -q/--quiet). (7) --version flag. (8) Exit codes: 0=success, 1=findings, 2=usage error. (9) Consider packaging as gh extension (gh extension install). (10) Support -- to end option processing. (11) 12-factor: prefer flags over args, help on no args.","title":"Align CLI with gh CLI extension conventions"}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-x6s","title":"Align CLI with GNU/POSIX conventions","description":"Audit and fix CLI UX against GNU Coding Standards, POSIX conventions, and 12 Factor CLI (https://medium.com/@jdxcode/12-factor-cli-apps-dd3c227a0e46): (1) Support short + long options for common flags: -o/--out, -l/--local, -q/--quiet, -v/--verbose, -V/--version. (2) Support -- to end option processing. (3) --version prints version and exits. (4) Errors go to stderr, output to stdout. (5) Exit codes: 0=success, 1=findings detected (with --exit-code), 2=usage error. (6) Respect NO_COLOR env var (https://no-color.org). (7) Consider accepting ORG via -O/--org as well as positional. (8) 12-factor: prefer flags over args, show help when run with no args, use tables for dense info, disposition chars for success/failure. Reference: GNU argp conventions, POSIX utility conventions, 12 Factor CLI Apps.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:22:31Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:23:16Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Research saved: .beads/research-gh-extension-conventions-2026-03-04.md. Key takeaways: (1) Repo must be named gh-\u003cname\u003e, executable at root named gh-\u003cname\u003e (no .sh). (2) No manifest needed for bash extensions. (3) Add gh-extension topic to repo for discoverability. (4) gh extension install . for local dev (symlink, instant edits). (5) Popular examples: meiji163/gh-notify (324+ stars) — just a single bash file at root."}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-3j8","title":"Package as gh CLI extension","description":"Ship as a gh CLI extension so users can install with 'gh extension install rapidfort/gh-actions-audit' and run 'gh actions-audit my-org'. Requirements: (1) Repo named gh-actions-audit (or alias/fork). (2) Root executable named gh-actions-audit (no .sh). (3) Shell-based extension — no manifest needed, gh auto-detects. (4) Users get: one-line install, runs from anywhere, 'gh extension upgrade' for updates. (5) Zero new dependencies — we already require gh. (6) Keep tests/CI working with renamed script. (7) Consider publishing to gh extension registry for discoverability. Research saved in .beads/ from agent. Pairs well with x6s (gh CLI conventions).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:27:02Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:27:02Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Decision: Option A — own JSONL schema as internal intermediate format, HDF v2 as output format (converter built later in card 0ku). Rationale: HDF v2 is control-centric (we're repo-centric), requires heavy boilerplate, no JSONL variant, hard to emit from bash 3.2. Our JSONL: flat, domain-specific, easy to emit, easy to grep/jq. HDF converter is ~50-80 lines deferred to 0ku."}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-h6c","title":"Evaluate HDF v2 as native JSONL schema vs output format","description":"Decision needed: should our JSONL intermediate format BE HDF v2, or should HDF v2 be one of many output renderers? Arguments for native HDF v2: single format, direct Heimdall import, compliance-ready. Arguments against: HDF v2 has a specific schema (profiles, controls, results) that may not map cleanly to our per-repo findings model — our data is org-level scanning, not per-system compliance. Research: read HDF v2 spec (saf.mitre.org), compare to our data shape, decide before implementing awb (JSONL schema). This decision blocks awb.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:33:27Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:33:27Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"[2026-03-04] Decision REVERSED: HDF v2 as native internal format (not just output). Our checks map 1:1 to HDF requirements. Wrapper for multi-repo/org scans. MD/CSV/XLSX are renderers from HDF, not separate paths."}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-h6c","title":"Evaluate HDF v2 as native JSONL schema vs output format","description":"Decision needed: should our JSONL intermediate format BE HDF v2, or should HDF v2 be one of many output renderers? new_value: {"description":"Create the HDF v2 baseline profile for gh-actions-audit. Each of our 28 detection checks becomes a requirement with: stable ID (GHA-001–028), STIG-style title/check/fix text, impact score, CCI tags, NIST 800-53 mappings, OWASP CI/CD category. Output: hdf-profile/requirements.yaml consumed by the renderer. Replaces the old 'define JSONL schema' plan — HDF v2 IS the schema.","title":"Define HDF v2 profile with requirement catalog"}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-awb","title":"Define JSONL output schema","description":"Design and document the JSONL schema for per-repo findings. One JSON object per line, one line per repo. Fields: repo name, org, all detection results with structured sub-fields (not pipe-delimited strings). Schema enables consistent rendering to md/csv/HDF and programmatic consumption. Write schema doc, validate with jq. Must be done before or with 55h (JSONL refactor).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:18:56Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:18:56Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Replace pipe-delimited temp file output with HDF v2 JSON generation. analyze_repo() produces per-requirement results. Phase 5 assembles wrapper with all repo objects + org-level object. MD/CSV renderers read from HDF structure instead of pipe-delimited strings. Add json_escape() helper. Keep bash 3.2 compat — use printf templates.","title":"Refactor output to HDF v2 native format"}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-55h","title":"Refactor to JSONL intermediate format","description":"Replace pipe-delimited temp files (TABLE_ROWS_FILE, TABLE_ROWS_CSV_FILE) with JSONL intermediate format. Each repo produces one JSON object with all findings. Report renderers (md, csv) read from JSONL. Benefits: decouples analysis from rendering, enables new output formats (HDF v2, xlsx), supports programmatic consumption (jq), eliminates field-index fragility. analyze_repo() outputs JSON instead of pipe-delimited. Phase 5 reads JSONL and renders. Acceptance: make check passes, existing md/csv output unchanged.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T04:17:50Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:17:50Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Since HDF v2 is now the native internal format, this card just adds the --hdf FILE CLI flag to write the assembled HDF v2 JSON to disk. Minimal work — the data is already in HDF v2 structure, just serialize and write.","title":"Add --hdf output flag to write HDF v2 file"}
old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-0ku","title":"Output report in HDF v2 format (MITRE heimdall-compatible)","description":"STRETCH GOAL — depends on CCI/NIST mapping (arv). Produce report output in HDF v2 (Heimdall Data Format) JSON, compatible with MITRE SAF Heimdall viewer. Reference: https://github.com/mitre/hdf-libs/tree/hdf-libs-development. HDF v2 wraps InSpec-style results with control metadata (NIST 800-53, CCI), status (passed/failed/not_applicable), and code_desc. Would allow feeding audit results into Heimdall for unified compliance dashboards. Implementation: generate JSON alongside md/csv using the CCI mappings from arv. May require jq or a small helper script for JSON construction.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"lippold@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T03:52:34Z","created_by":"Aaron Lippold","updated_at":"2026-03-04T03:52:34Z"}

old_value: {"id":"gh-action-security-audit-arv","title":"Map tests and detections to CCI and NIST 800-53 controls","description":"Associate each detection check (prt, ic, unpinned, expr injection, workflow_run, self-hosted, dangerous perms, org secrets) with the relevant CCI (Control Correlation Identifier) and NIST 800-53 controls. Add a mapping table to README or TESTING.md showing: detection → CCI → NIST control (e.g., unpinned actions → CCI-003161 → CM-7, expression injection → CCI-001310 → SI-10). Tag bats tests with the CCI reference in comments.
